LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT JENNIFER TAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DEFAMATION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ("FACC") DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH THAT THE STATEMENTS WERE REASONABLY UNDERSTOOD BY A THIRD PERSON TO BE ABOUT CROSS-PLAINTIFF OR THAT THE THIRD PERSON REASONABLY UNDERSTOOD THE STATEMENT TO DAMAGE CROSS-PLAINTIFF'S BUSINESS REPUTATION. (SEE REGALIA V. NETHERCUTT COLLECTION (2009) 172 CAL.APP.4TH 361, 368; "TO BE ACTIONABLE PER SE, A DEFAMATORY STATEMENT MUST TEND 'DIRECTLY' TO INJURE THE PERSON DEFAMED IN RESPECT TO HIS OFFICE, PROFESSION, TRADE OR BUSINESS.) AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH CROSS-DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN CONDUCT "SO OUTRAGEOUS IN CHARACTER, AND SO EXTREME IN DEGREE, AS TO GO BEYOND ALL POSSIBLE BOUNDS OF DECENCY, AND TO BE REGARDED AS ATROCIOUS, AND UTTERLY INTOLERABLE IN A CIVILIZED COMMUNITY." (COCHRAN V. COCHRAN (1998) 65 CAL.APP.4TH 488, 496.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE FACC PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING EACH ELEMENT. (SEE HUGHES V. PAIR (2009) 46 CAL.4TH 1035, 1050-51; FACC, 27, 62-71.) AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SEC. 17200 ET SEQ., THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACC DOES NOT ADEQUATELY PLEAD AN "UNLAWFUL" BUSINESS PRACTICE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC.AND NRT LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER MS. TANVERAKUL WAS ACTING WITHIN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF HER EMPLOYMENT WITH DEFENDANT WHEN THE ALLEGED TORTIOUS CONDUCT OCCURRED. AT 9:23-11:22 AND 31:12-32:21, MR. CHUNG EXPLAINS THAT HE CONTACTED MS. TANVERAKUL BECAUSE HE BELIEVED THAT SHE MAY HAVE MADE SOME SORT OF ERROR WITH REGARD TO THE PURCHASE OF THE 325 26TH AVE PROPERTY. (SEE ALSO LIEU DEPO, 54:9-16). THAT CONTACT WAS DIRECTLY RELATED TO DEFENDANT'S BUSINESS AND A TRIER OF FACT COULD FIND THAT THE SUBSEQUENT MEETING WHERE THE ALLEGED DEFAMATION AND OTHER TORTS OCCURRED WAS A NATURAL OUTGROWTH OF THE BROKER/AGENT BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE OF CC 47(C) APPLIES. THE COURT LIBERALLY CONSTRUES PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE TRIER OF FACT CAN INFER MS. TANVERAKUL'S MALICE (ILL WILL/RECKLESS DISREGARD OF THE TRUTH) FROM THE FOLLOWING: (1) MS. TANVERAKUL COULD HAVE HARBORED ILL WILL BECAUSE SHE WAS ACCUSED OF BUNGLING THE REAL ESTATE TRANSACTION BASED PLAINTIFF'S FLYER; (2) MS. TANVERAKUL SCREAMED AT PLAINTIFF AND THREATENED TO REPORT HER TO THE REAL ESTATE BOARD; AND (3) MS. TANVERAKUL HAD A FINANCIAL INCENTIVE TO DRIVE A WEDGE BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND MRS. LIEU. (TAN DEPO., OPPOSITION TO UMF 31). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185 =302/EG

LAW AND MOTION 302, JOINDER AND PARTIAL JOINDER OF DEFENDANT NOUR TANVERAKUL IN DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC. AND NRT LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE OF CC 47(C) APPLIES. THE COURT LIBERALLY CONSTRUES PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE TRIER OF FACT CAN INFER MS. TANVERAKUL'S MALICE (ILL WILL/RECKLESS DISREGARD OF THE TRUTH) FROM THE FOLLOWING: (1) MS. TANVERAKUL COULD HAVE HARBORED ILL WILL BECAUSE SHE WAS ACCUSED OF BUNGLING THE REAL ESTATE TRANSACTION BASED PLAINTIFF'S FLYER; (2) MS. TANVERAKUL SCREAMED AT PLAINTIFF AND THREATENED TO REPORT HER TO THE REAL ESTATE BOARD; AND (3) MS. TANVERAKUL HAD A FINANCIAL INCENTIVE TO DRIVE A WEDGE BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND MRS. LIEU. (TAN DEPO., OPPOSITION TO UMF 31). JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185 =302/EG

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Partial Joinder And Partial Joinder
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MAUREEN PETTIBONE RYAN & ATTY. JOHN-PAUL S. DEOL OF DHILLON & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 433-1700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JASON PUTNAM GORDON, TEL. NO. 986-7200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT NOUR EL HOUDA TANVERKAUL;
ATTY. RICHARD C. RAINES OF GAGEN MC COY, TEL. NO. (925) 837-0585 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; JOINDER AND PARTIAL JOINDER OF DEFENDANT NOUR TANVERAKUL IN DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC. AND NRT LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The court denies the motion for summary judgment. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the common interest privilege of CC 47(c) applies. The court liberally construes plaintiff's evidence on summary judgment. The trier of fact can infer Ms. Tanverakul's malice (ill will/reckless disregard of the truth) from the following: (1) Ms. Tanverakul could have harbored ill will because she was accused of bungling the real estate transaction based plaintiff's flyer; (2) Ms. Tanverakul screamed at plaintiff and threatened to report her to the real estate board; and (3) Ms. Tanverakul had a financial incentive to drive a wedge between plaintiff and Mrs. Lieu. (Tan depo., Opposition to UMF 31). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/eg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice
Off Calendar Sep-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MAUREEN PETTIBONE RYAN & ATTY. JOHN-PAUL S. DEOL OF DHILLON & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 433-1700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JASON PUTNAM GORDON, TEL. NO. 986-7200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT NOUR EL HOUDA TANVERKAUL;
ATTY. RICHARD C. RAINES OF GAGEN MC COY, TEL. NO. (925) 837-0585 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NRT WEST, INC.and NRT LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The court denies the motion for summary judgment. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Ms. Tanverakul was acting within the course and scope of her employment with defendant when the alleged tortious conduct occurred. At 9:23-11:22 and 31:12-32:21, Mr. Chung explains that he contacted Ms. Tanverakul because he believed that she may have made some sort of error with regard to the purchase of the 325 26th Ave property. (See also Lieu depo, 54:9-16). That contact was directly related to defendant's business and a trier of fact could find that the subsequent meeting where the alleged defamation and other torts occurred was a natural outgrowth of the broker/agent business relationship. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the common interest privilege of CC 47(c) applies. The court liberally construes plaintiff's evidence on summary judgment. The trier of fact can infer Ms. Tanverakul's malice (ill will/reckless disregard of the truth) from the following: (1) Ms. Tanverakul could have harbored ill will because she was accused of bungling the real estate transaction based plaintiff's flyer; (2) Ms. Tanverakul screamed at plaintiff and threatened to report her to the real estate board; and (3) Ms. Tanverakul had a financial incentive to drive a wedge between plaintiff and Mrs. Lieu. (Tan depo., Opposition to UMF 31). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT JENNIFER TAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. As to the first cause of action for defamation, the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not plead facts that establish that the statements were reasonably understood by a third person to be about Cross-Plaintiff or that the third person reasonably understood the statement to damage Cross-Plaintiff's business reputation. (See Regalia v. Nethercutt Collection (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 361, 368; "to be actionable per se, a defamatory statement must tend 'directly' to injure the person defamed in respect to his office, profession, trade or business.) As to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The FACC does not plead facts that establish Cross-Defendant engaged in conduct "so outrageous in character, and so extreme in degree, as to go beyond all possible bounds of decency, and to be regarded as atrocious, and utterly intolerable in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 496.) As to the third cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, the demurrer is overruled. The FACC pleads facts establishing each element. (See Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1050-51; FACC, 27, 62-71.) As to the fourth cause of action for violation of business and professions code sec. 17200 et seq., the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The FACC does not adequately plead an "unlawful" business practice. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
